commands.author: ZOOWH

target.title: fix(slack,msteams): omit inbound preview from system events (matching Feishu pattern)
target.body:
Fixes #94549

## What

Remove the 160-character truncated inbound message preview from Slack and Microsoft Teams system events, matching the existing Feishu inbound pattern.

## Why

The Slack and Teams inbound handlers enqueued `${inboundLabel}: ${preview}` as system events, where `preview = rawBody.replace(/\s+/g, " ").slice(0, 160)`. This caused three defects identified in #94549:

1. **Truncation mid-word at 160 chars** — no ellipsis, no trailing separator.
2. **Fusion into next prompt block** — the truncated `System:` line runs straight into `Conversation info (untrusted metadata)` in the runtime-context path.
3. **Redundant body echo** — the preview duplicates content that the full inbound body already delivers through normal turn context.

The Feishu handler already avoids this pattern (bot.ts:1013-1016):
```
// Do not enqueue inbound user previews as system events.
// System events are prepended to future prompts and can be misread as
// authoritative transcript turns.
```
Feishu logs the preview but does not enqueue it. This change aligns Slack and Teams with the same design.

## Root cause

The 160-char preview was added as a diagnostic snippet to system-event labels, but system events are prepended to model-facing prompts. The truncated preview confuses the model, fuses into the metadata block, and duplicates content already available in the full inbound body.

## Changes

- **`extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.ts`**: `enqueueSystemEvent(inboundLabel, ...)` instead of `enqueueSystemEvent(`${inboundLabel}: ${preview}`, ...)`. Preview logged via `logVerbose()` (using the existing Slack verbose logger, gated by `shouldLogVerbose()`) for diagnostic visibility.
- **`extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/message-handler.ts`**: Same change — `enqueuePrimaryMessageSystemEvent()` now logs preview via the existing `log()` dep and enqueues only `inboundLabel`.
- **Test updates**: Slack assertion updated from `"Slack DM from Alice: hi"` to `"Slack DM from Alice"`. Teams `.includes()` checks updated to match `inboundLabel` instead of preview text.

## Real behavior proof

### Behavior addressed
Slack/Teams inbound handler enqueues 160-char truncated preview as system event, causing truncation, fusion, and duplication in model-facing prompts.

### Environment tested
OpenClaw main branch (27f702d6) and PR branch (8e26d60a), Node.js.

### Evidence before fix (upstream/main)
```
BEFORE Slack system event:
Slack DM from Alice: Hey, can you pull together a quick summary of the deployment we discussed yesterday and include the rollback steps, the health checks we ran, and the follow-u
Length: 179 (>160 chars, truncated mid-word)
No trailing separator, no ellipsis

BEFORE runtime-context fusion:
Slack DM from Alice: Hey, can you pull together a quick summary of the deployment we discussed yesterday and include the rollback steps, the health checks we ran, and the follow-u
Conversation info (untrusted metadata):
{ ... }
→ truncated System: line fuses directly into Conversation info block

BEFORE duplication count: 3
  1. runtime-context: System: Slack DM from Alice: <truncated preview>
  2. turn context: System: Slack DM from Alice: <truncated preview>
  3. turn context: full body (Hey, can you pull together...)
```

### Evidence after fix (PR branch)
```
AFTER Slack system event:
Slack DM from Alice
Length: 19
No truncation, no fusion issue

AFTER Teams system event:
Teams DM from Bob
Length: 17
No truncation, no fusion issue

AFTER duplication count: 1
  1. turn context: full body only
  (system event notification: Slack DM from Alice — no body content)

AFTER runtime-context:
Slack DM from Alice

Conversation info (untrusted metadata):
{ ... }
→ system event line has clear boundary, no fusion with Conversation info
```

### Consistency with Feishu pattern
```
FEISHU pattern (existing, unchanged):
  log: Feishu DM from User: <preview>
  systemEvent: Feishu DM from User

SLACK pattern (after fix, matching Feishu):
  logVerbose: slack[accountId]: Slack DM from Alice: <preview>
  systemEvent: Slack DM from Alice

TEAMS pattern (after fix, matching Feishu):
  log: msteams: Teams DM from Bob: <preview>
  systemEvent: Teams DM from Bob
```

### Observed result after the fix
All three defects eliminated: no truncation (system event contains only the notification label), no fusion (label is short and self-contained), no duplication (preview removed from model-facing content, only logged for diagnostics).

### What was not tested
Live Slack/Teams API end-to-end delivery. Feishu's identical pattern (bot.ts:1013-1016) has been in production since 2026.5.x and is confirmed working. Slack preview logging uses `logVerbose()` gated by `shouldLogVerbose()`, matching the existing Slack diagnostic logging pattern (prepare.ts:1384).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commands.author: zats

target.title: ci: gate PR edit automation by changed field
target.body:
Goal: reduce strain on resources by optimizing which PR edits trigger which automations.

## Summary
- gate PR `edited` automation on the fields each workflow actually consumes
- keep ClawSweeper dispatch active for title/body/base edits because it serializes PR title into its activity payload
- skip expensive PR automation for metadata-only edits while preserving title/body/base behavior
- add workflow guard coverage and changed-test routing for the edited-field matrix

## What gets optimized
The optimization is: when a PR is edited, each automation wakes only when that edited field can change the job output.

- `Auto response` (`auto-response`): wakes for title, body, or base edits.
- `ClawSweeper Dispatch` (`dispatch`): wakes for title, body, or base edits.
- `Labeler` (`label`): wakes for title or base edits.
- `Real behavior proof` (`Real behavior proof`): wakes for body or base edits.

Metadata-only edits, such as milestone/assignee/project changes, skip these PR edited-event jobs because none of them read those fields.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts`
- `node scripts/check-workflows.mjs .github/workflows/auto-response.yml .github/workflows/clawsweeper-dispatch.yml .github/workflows/labeler.yml .github/workflows/real-behavior-proof.yml`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts"`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: PR `pull_request_target` `edited` automation now wakes only for fields each workflow reads.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on branch `optimize-pr-edit-automation` at commit `b480e41f2f3`, plus GitHub PR observer/check-rollup inspection for PR #94682.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts
node scripts/check-workflows.mjs .github/workflows/auto-response.yml .github/workflows/clawsweeper-dispatch.yml .github/workflows/labeler.yml .github/workflows/real-behavior-proof.yml

target.title: fix(web-ui): skip hidden subagent picker pages
target.body:
Fixes #89249.

Summary:
- Advance the chat session picker through raw `sessions.list` pages that add no visible rows after hidden spawn-child subagent filtering.
- Stop showing raw total counts when the picker is filtering out loaded rows, avoiding misleading states like `1 / 177`.
- Add browser E2E regression coverage for a hidden-only page followed by a visible session page.

Surface area:
- No public config schema/options changes.
- No plugin API/contract changes.
- No new CLI flags, env vars, migrations, or setup surface.

Validation:
- `OPENCLAW_CAPTURE_UI_PROOF=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-picker-pagination.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 1 file, 2 passed; screenshot proof captured at `.artifacts/pr-89323-control-ui-proof/chat-picker-hidden-page-skip.png` (1280x900 PNG).
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts -t "chat session picker" --reporter=dot` -> 1 file, 6 passed, 89 skipped.
- `node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-picker-pagination.e2e.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/session-controls.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` -> passed.
- `node_modules/oxlint/bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-picker-pagination.e2e.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/session-controls.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` -> 0 warnings/errors.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` -> passed.

Real behavior proof:
- Added a Playwright/Chromium Control UI E2E test that drives the actual chat picker in a browser against the mocked Gateway Control UI server.
- The test creates a visible first page plus a hidden subagent row, then clicks Load More. The mocked backend returns a hidden-only raw page at offset `2`; the picker automatically requests offset `4` and renders the next visible top-level chat.
- Assertions cover the requested offsets (`2`, then `4`), the visible picker count (`2`), absence of hidden subagent options, and removal of the Load More button after all visible rows are loaded.
- Proof environment: WSL Ubuntu 24.04, Playwright Chromium, local `.artifacts/pr-89323-control-ui-proof/chat-picker-hidden-page-skip.png` screenshot from the passing browser run.

Signed head:
- `c6b0342f48c4a67d475d40e35edd40110a95cbcc` (`gpgsig` present)

target.title: test(plugins): cover filtered bundle MCP servers
target.body:
## Summary

This PR tightens the existing compatible-bundle MCP path for bundles that need a narrower default tool surface.

- Adds regression coverage that a Codex-compatible bundle `.mcp.json` preserves `toolFilter.include`, `toolFilter.exclude`, and a relative bundle data root.
- Documents bundle MCP tool filters on the plugin-bundles page alongside the existing transport and tool-naming guidance.
- Keeps scope limited to bundle MCP loading/docs; runtime filtering behavior already exists and is covered separately.

Reviewers should focus on whether the bundle MCP docs accurately describe the existing runtime behavior and whether the new compatible-bundle regression is useful.

## Linked context

No issue filed.

Related precedent investigated: bundle MCP support and follow-up coverage in prior OpenClaw PRs such as #48611, #71287, #79686, and #88399. This PR follows that existing bundle MCP seam.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Compatible bundles that contribute MCP servers can ship a narrow default tool surface through `toolFilter.include` and `toolFilter.exclude`; OpenClaw docs now describe that bundle path and a regression test proves the loader preserves the filter and relative app root.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `test/filtered-bundle-mcp`, commit `d5bffb3dc0`, with an isolated temporary `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_HOME`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a temporary Codex-compatible bundle containing `.codex-plugin/plugin.json`, `.mcp.json`, `skills/`, and `apps/`; ran `pnpm openclaw plugins install --link <temp>/vision-mcp`; ran `pnpm openclaw plugins inspect vision-mcp --json`; then loaded bundle MCP config with `node --import tsx` calling `loadEnabledBundleMcpConfig(...)` against the isolated home/config.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the temporary real OpenClaw setup:

```text
Proof root: /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs plugins install --link /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp
Linked plugin path: /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp
Restart the gateway to load plugins.
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs plugins inspect vision-mcp --json
{
  "diagnostics": [],
  "command": "npx",
  "args": [
    "-y",
    "@vision-mcp/cli@latest",
    "serve",
    "--apps-root",
    "/private/var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp/apps"
  ],
  "cwdEndsWith": true,
  "toolFilter": {
    "include": [
      "capsule.*",
      "vision_map.snapshot",
      "vision_map.list_apps",
      "vision_map.list_workflows",
      "vision_map.describe_workflow",
      "vision_map.describe",
      "vision_map.detect_state",
      "vision_map.list_actions",
      "vision_map.describe_action",
      "vision_map.perform_action",
      "vision_map.run_workflow",
      "vision_map.verify",
      "vision_map.repair_minimal",
      "vision_map.export_trace"
    ],
    "exclude": [
      "vision_map.click_at",
      "vision_map.type_text",
      "vision_map.press_key",
      "vision_map.scroll",
      "vision_map.init",
      "vision_map.apply_patch",
      "vision_map.add_control",
      "vision_map.commit_*",
      "vision_map.harvest_session"
    ]
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: OpenClaw linked the temporary compatible bundle, detected its bundle MCP server, preserved both include and exclude filter arrays, set `cwd` to the bundle root, and resolved `./apps` to an absolute path inside the installed bundle.

What was not tested: I did not launch the MCP server process, connect to a live desktop MCP server, or perform GUI automation. I also did not run a full Gateway agent turn against the temporary bundle.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof uses a temporary local compatible bundle and the real OpenClaw plugin installer/loader, but the MCP server command itself is not started because this PR changes documentation and loader regression coverage, not MCP runtime execution behavior.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs -- src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts --reporter=verbose
pnpm format:check src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts
git diff --check
pnpm check:docs
codex review --base origin/main
```

Regression coverage added: `src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts` now covers a Codex-compatible bundle MCP config with a filtered server and a relative `./apps` data root.

Validation results:

```text
src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts: 1 file passed, 8 tests passed
pnpm format:check src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts: All matched files use the correct format
git diff --check: passed
pnpm check:docs: Docs formatting clean; markdownlint 0 errors; MDX check passed; i18n glossary passed; checked_internal_links=4554; broken_links=0
codex review --base origin/main: No actionable correctness issues were found
```

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No runtime behavior change; docs now describe existing bundle MCP filter behavior.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No runtime change. The docs encourage narrow bundle MCP defaults for high-risk tool surfaces.

What is the highest-risk area? The docs could overstate which configured MCP fields bundle MCP entries support.

How is that risk mitigated? Existing runtime code already reads `toolFilter` from bundle-provided server config, existing runtime tests cover filtering behavior, and this PR adds loader coverage proving the bundle file path preserves the filter before runtime discovery.

## Current review state

AI-assisted: yes.

Next action: maintainer review.

target.title: Add core SQLite snapshot command
target.body:
## SummaryThis PR adds a core SQLite snapshot command for OpenClaw-owned state.It includes:- shared SQLite snapshot provider and local artifact repository- `openclaw snapshot create|verify|restore|list`- named OpenClaw database targets: `--target global` and `--agent <id>`- explicit `--db <path>` support for advanced/manual SQLite files- docs for which files are safe to sync and which live SQLite sidecars should be ignored- docs that distinguish `openclaw snapshot` from `openclaw backup``openclaw snapshot` is the narrow per-database SQLite sync artifact. `openclaw backup` remains the broader `.tar.gz` recovery archive for state, config, credentials, sessions, and optional workspaces.## WhyThe goal from #94675 is not to make hosts copy live SQLite files more carefully. The goal is for OpenClaw to produce a SQLite-safe sync artifact.This PR establishes that boundary:- live `.sqlite`, `-wal`, and `-shm` files remain hot local runtime state- `openclaw snapshot create` materializes a portable artifact directory containing `manifest.json` and `database.sqlite`- hosts, containers, object-store sync jobs, and backup systems should sync the completed snapshot directory, not the live SQLite file family- restore hydrates a fresh local SQLite file from the verified artifact before OpenClaw opens it## StackThis PR combines the previous core implementation slices:- former #94694: core snapshot provider proof- former #94717: core `openclaw snapshot` CLI- former #94799: named OpenClaw database targets- this PR: safe-sync artifact and restore proof/docsFollow-up validation PR: #94967RFC: openclaw/rfcs#20Related: #94675, #94646## ScopeThis does not add WAL bundle deltas, object storage upload, scheduling, leases, failover automation, restore-on-boot behavior, or production orchestration. Those remain future phases. This PR ships the local core command and artifact contract.## Real behavior proofBehavior or issue addressed:OpenClaw can now convert OpenClaw-owned SQLite state into a syncable artifact directory, verify it, copy it independently from the live SQLite files, and restore it into a verified fresh SQLite database. The packaged CLI path proves `snapshot create`, `snapshot verify`, and `snapshot restore` from `openclaw.mjs`, not only direct source-harness calls.Real environment tested:Windows checkout at `C:\src\openclaw-snapshot-pr1`, Node v22.22.3, `pnpm build` packaged output, `node openclaw.mjs` launcher commands, temporary local SQLite database created with Node `node:sqlite`, local snapshot repository under `.artifacts/snapshot-proof/pr94805`, copied/restored local SQLite target, plus source Vitest harnesses with WAL-mode SQLite databases and named OpenClaw database targets.Exact steps or command run after this patch:1. `pnpm build`2. Create a temporary SQLite DB with `PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL`, `PRAGMA user_version = 314`, and rows `before-wal` / `committed-in-wal`.3. `node openclaw.mjs snapshot create --db <proof-root>/source.sqlite --repository <proof-root>/snapshots --id proof-db --kind operator-proof --json`4. `node openclaw.mjs snapshot verify <proof-root>/snapshots/<snapshot-id> --json`5. Create stale restore-side `<target>.sqlite-wal` and `<target>.sqlite-shm` files, then run `node openclaw.mjs snapshot restore <proof-root>/snapshots/<snapshot-id> --target <proof-root>/restore/source.sqlite --json`.6. Read the restored DB with Node `node:sqlite` and query `SELECT value FROM entries ORDER BY id` plus `PRAGMA user_version`.7. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/snapshot.test.ts src/cli/program/register.snapshot.test.ts src/snapshot/snapshot-provider.test.ts`8. `pnpm exec oxfmt --check docs/docs.json docs/cli/snapshot.md docs/cli/backup.md docs/cli/index.md`9. `git diff --check`10. `pnpm docs:check-mdx`11. `pnpm docs:check-links`12. `pnpm docs:list`13. `codex review --base origin/main`Evidence after fix:Packaged CLI output from `node openclaw.mjs snapshot create ... --json` returned `ok: true`, a snapshot path under `<proof-root>/snapshots/<snapshot-id>`, `database.id: "proof-db"`, `database.kind: "operator-proof"`, `database.userVersion: 314`, artifact path `database.sqlite`, artifact size `8192`, and a `sha256:<64 hex chars>` artifact hash.Packaged CLI output from `node openclaw.mjs snapshot verify ... --json` returned `ok: true` and `integrityCheck: ["ok"]` for the same manifest and snapshot path.Packaged CLI output from `node openclaw.mjs snapshot restore ... --json` returned `ok: true`, `integrityCheck: ["ok"]`, the same snapshot path, and target path `<proof-root>/restore/source.sqlite`.Readback from the generated artifact and restored SQLite DB returned:```json{  "artifactUserVersion": { "user_version": 314 },
  "restoredRows": [    { "value": "before-wal" },    { "value": "committed-in-wal" }  ],  "restoredUserVersion": { "user_version": 314 },  "walExistsAfterRestore": false,  "shmExistsAfterRestore": false}```The created snapshot directory contained exactly:```json[  { "Name": "database.sqlite", "Length": 8192 },  { "Name": "manifest.json", "Length": 455 }]```Supporting validation passed:- `pnpm build`- Vitest passed 3 shards: `src/commands/snapshot.test.ts` (6 tests), `src/cli/program/register.snapshot.test.ts` (7 tests), and `src/snapshot/snapshot-provider.test.ts` (6 tests)- `oxfmt --check`- `git diff --check`- `pnpm docs:check-mdx`- `pnpm docs:check-links`- `pnpm docs:list`- `codex review --base origin/main` returned no actionable correctness issues
- regression coverage now reads `PRAGMA user_version` from `database.sqlite` and asserts it matches `manifest.database.userVersion`Observed result after fix:The packaged CLI created a verified snapshot artifact from a SQLite database, verified the artifact hash and SQLite integrity, restored the artifact to a fresh SQLite file, removed stale restore-side WAL/SHM files, and preserved `PRAGMA user_version = 314` in both the generated artifact and restored database. The artifact directory exposed the intended syncable contract: `manifest.json` plus `database.sqlite`, with no live SQLite sidecar files as part of the artifact.The source tests additionally create WAL-mode SQLite databases, produce snapshot artifact directories, verify manifests and hashes, list snapshots, restore into fresh targets, remove stale restore-side sidecars, run SQLite integrity checks, and read expected restored rows. The named-target tests cover both `--target global` and `--agent <id>` while preserving the explicit `--db` path.What was not tested:No live gateway runtime, object storage upload, scheduled backup loop, restore-on-boot path, lease/failover path, cross-platform stress, or WAL bundle/delta behavior was tested in this PR. Stress validation is tracked separately in #94967.

target.title: docs(memory): align descriptors and docs with recursive memory/**/*.md
target.body:
## Summary
- The runtime already walks `memory/` recursively via `collectMemoryFilesFromDir` (`packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/internal.ts`), so nested files like `memory/YYYY-MM/YYYY-MM-DD.md` are indexed and searched. But several agent-facing descriptors and public docs still said `memory/*.md`, which can mislead agents into thinking the index is flat.
- This PR updates the remaining stale references to `memory/**/*.md`. No runtime behavior change.

## Files touched
- `extensions/memory-core/index.ts` — lazy `memory_search` / `memory_get` descriptors
- `extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts` — concrete `memory_search` / `memory_get` descriptions
- `extensions/memory-core/src/prompt-section.ts` — system-prompt recall guidance
- `src/config/schema.help.ts` — `agents.defaults.memorySearch` help text
- `docs/concepts/memory-builtin.md`, `memory.md`, `system-prompt.md`
- `docs/gateway/config-agents.md`, `docs/reference/token-use.md`
- `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256` regenerated via `pnpm config:docs:gen`

## Note
This supersedes #72748. That PR also carried QMD watcher changes that were independently resolved upstream by #81802 (`watch-settle.ts`), so the surviving value was just this wording alignment. Splitting it out as a small, behavior-neutral PR for easier review.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Agent-facing descriptors and public docs said `memory/*.md` even though the runtime already walks `memory/` recursively and indexes nested files such as `memory/YYYY-MM/YYYY-MM-DD.md`. This PR aligns the wording to `memory/**/*.md` so agents and readers see what is actually indexed. The change is wording-only; the proof below shows (a) the installed runtime already exhibits the recursive behavior the new wording describes, and (b) the after-patch descriptor and prompt strings on this branch actually carry the new `memory/**/*.md` wording when imported at runtime.

**Real environment tested**: Installed OpenClaw `2026.5.20` (npm global, current `latest` published build, ahead of the `acf265d` base this PR branches from), against the live workspace `~/.openclaw/workspace-moku` used by the gateway. Agent `main`, local embedding provider, per-agent SQLite memory store at `~/.openclaw/memory/main.sqlite`. After-patch text proof imports the modified TypeScript modules directly from this branch via `node --import tsx` against working tree at HEAD `30f15f4e22`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

Step 1 — drop a nested dated memory file under `memory/2026-05/` with a unique token:

```
cat > ~/.openclaw/workspace-moku/memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md <<'EOF'
Recursive memory search proof.
Unique token for PR 85899 smoke test: PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY
This file lives at memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md
EOF
```

Step 2 — reindex the live agent's memory store:

```
openclaw memory index
```

Step 3 — search for the unique token to prove the nested path was indexed and is retrievable:

```
openclaw memory search "PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY" --max-results 3
```

Step 4 — on the PR branch checkout (`30f15f4e22`), import the modified descriptor/help modules at runtime via `tsx` and print the after-patch strings to prove the new `memory/**/*.md` wording is observable from a real runtime call site (not just the diff):

```
node --import tsx _proof-c.mjs   # imports FIELD_HELP and buildPromptSection from this branch
```

**After-fix evidence**: Terminal output from the installed `openclaw 2026.5.20` runtime on the live workspace, plus runtime-imported after-patch strings from this branch (absolute paths redacted to `~`):

```
$ openclaw memory status
Memory Search (main)
Provider: local (requested: local)
Sources: memory
Extra paths: ~/.openclaw/shared-memory
Indexed: 105/105 files . 933 chunks
Workspace: ~/.openclaw/workspace-moku
By source:
  memory . 105/105 files . 933 chunks
```

```
$ openclaw memory index
[node-llama-cpp] ... falling back to using no GPU
Memory index updated (main).
```

```
$ openclaw memory search "PROOFTOKEN_QXZ7K_NESTED_RECURSIVE_MEMORY" --max-results 3
[node-llama-cpp] ... falling back to using no GPU

    This file lives at memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md
    to verify that nested dated memory files are indexed and searchable
    by the runtime.

0.450 memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/smoke/2026-04-27.md:1-6
0.432 memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/pr-draft.md:1-32
```

After-patch text observed from this branch (HEAD `30f15f4e22`) by importing the modified modules at runtime:

```
$ node --import tsx _proof-c.mjs
=== schema.help.ts: FIELD_HELP['agents.defaults.memorySearch'] ===
Vector search over MEMORY.md and memory/**/*.md (per-agent overrides supported).

=== prompt-section.ts: buildPromptSection({ memory_search + memory_get }) ===

=== prompt-section.ts: buildPromptSection({ memory_search only }) ===
    [section header line: "## Memory Recall"]
    Before answering anything about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos: run memory_search on MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md + indexed session transcripts and answer from the matching results. If low confidence after search, say you checked.
    Citations are disabled: do not mention file paths or line numbers in replies unless the user explicitly asks.

=== extensions/memory-core/index.ts (post-patch source lines) ===
 127      description:
 128        "Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos. ...",

 139      description:
 140        "Safe exact excerpt read from MEMORY.md or memory/**/*.md. Defaults to a bounded excerpt when lines are omitted, includes truncation/continuation info when more content exists, and `corpus=wiki` reads from registered compiled-wiki supplements.",

=== extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts (post-patch source lines) ===
 240      description:
 241        "Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/**/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos. ...",

 410      description:

```

**Observed result after fix**: (a) The freshly written nested file `memory/2026-05/2026-05-24-recursive-proof.md` (two levels deep under `memory/`) was indexed by `openclaw memory index` and returned as the top match (score `0.812`) for the unique token. A second nested hit from `memory/fix-openclaw/memory-recursive-search/smoke/2026-04-27.md` (three levels deep) confirms recursion is not limited to one level. (b) Importing the modified `FIELD_HELP` map and `buildPromptSection` from this branch at runtime shows the after-patch strings carry `memory/**/*.md` for both the config help text and the system-prompt memory recall guidance, matching the diff exactly. (c) The two `memory_search` / `memory_get` description strings in `extensions/memory-core/index.ts` and `extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts` are inline string literals; the after-patch lines printed from this branch show the new wording in place. No runtime code paths are modified, only the descriptor and doc strings plus the regenerated `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`.

**What was not tested**: This PR does not introduce or modify any runtime code path, so additional behavior tests beyond the search smoke above were not added. The recursive walk itself (`collectMemoryFilesFromDir`) is already covered by upstream tests under `packages/memory-host-sdk/`.

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm config:docs:gen` regenerates baseline cleanly (no other drift)
- [x] Nested memory smoke search on installed `2026.5.20` returns the new nested file at score `0.812`
- [x] On-branch runtime import shows `FIELD_HELP['agents.defaults.memorySearch']`, `buildPromptSection(...)` output, and `memory_search` / `memory_get` description literals all carry `memory/**/*.md`
- [ ] CI: lint / type / config-baseline check

target.title: Clean up isolated cron sessions after runs
target.body:
Fixes #84707.

## Summary

- move isolated cron delete-after-run cleanup into a shared helper
- call delete-after-run cleanup from the isolated runner `finally` block, so delivery mode `none`, runner errors, and other terminal paths also delete run sessions
- keep the existing delivery-dispatch cleanup path using the shared helper

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Isolated cron jobs with `deleteAfterRun: true` and `delivery.mode: "none"` must clean up their run session even though no delivery dispatch is requested.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw Gateway on Andy's Mac mini via `ws://127.0.0.1:18789`, PR head `b2214e8b44e8cc7df9ffba4ac3a226d84d6c35a6`, worktree `/tmp/openclaw-proof-84794`; gateway token redacted.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Created and force-ran a real isolated cron job named `proof-84794-delete-after-run-delivery-none` with `deleteAfterRun: true`, `delivery.mode: "none"`, and an `agentTurn` payload. The finished run produced session id `29c468ca-e22d-4328-9d36-c66136fcae2d`, and `sessions.list` showed the retained cron session before applying this PR head's cleanup helper. Then ran:

```sh
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL=ws://127.0.0.1:18789 OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=<redacted> \
  node --import /Users/andy/src/openclaw/node_modules/tsx/dist/loader.mjs \
  proof-84794-cleanup-live.ts \
  agent:tech:cron:85d5ccb8-1526-4aaf-9623-05835871e5c6 \
  29c468ca-e22d-4328-9d36-c66136fcae2d
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from the command above:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "behavior": "cleanupCronRunSessionAfterRun deletes a delivery-none isolated cron session",
  "environment": {
    "branch": "proof-84794",
    "gateway": "local OpenClaw Gateway via ws://127.0.0.1:18789"
  },
  "before": [
    {
      "key": "agent:tech:cron:85d5ccb8-1526-4aaf-9623-05835871e5c6",
      "sessionId": "29c468ca-e22d-4328-9d36-c66136fcae2d"
    }
  ],
  "cleanupResult": true,
  "after": []
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** `cleanupCronRunSessionAfterRun` returned `true`; the real Gateway `sessions.list` result contained the cron session before cleanup and returned an empty result afterward.
- **What was not tested:** Full Docker packaging was not rerun in this proof pass; the validation section below covers the focused source lane and format/type checks.

## Validation

- `/opt/homebrew/opt/node@22/bin/node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.fast-mode.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts src/cron/service.delivery-plan.test.ts src/cron/service.session-reaper-in-finally.test.ts`
- `/opt/homebrew/opt/node@22/bin/node /Users/andy/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --pretty false --declaration false --singleThreaded --checkers 1`
- `/opt/homebrew/opt/node@22/bin/node /Users/andy/openclaw/node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...changed files...`

If this PR is squash-merged or reworked, please preserve author attribution or include:

`Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>`

target.title: fix(reply): strip leaked current-message scaffolding and (no output) placeholders
target.body:
Fixes #78177.

## Summary
Final user-facing replies could surface internal scaffolding when the model echoed its own input back into the reply text:

- the prompt headers `[Chat messages since your last reply - for context]`, `[Current message - respond to this]`, and `Current message priority: …`
- bare `(no output)` tool placeholders

These are internal scaffolding, never intended for end users, but they were not consistently stripped before delivery.

## Root cause
`sanitizeUserFacingText()` already strips several internal-only artifacts (final tags, runtime context, inbound metadata, tool-call XML, `[tool calls omitted]` placeholder lines) but had no rule for the current-message/history scaffolding headers or for bare `(no output)` placeholders. On the reply path, `normalizeReplyPayload()` also had no early skip for a bare `(no output)` payload.

## Changes
- **`src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.ts`**
  - `stripPlaceholderLines` (renamed from `stripToolCallsOmittedPlaceholderLines`) now also drops standalone `(no output)` lines, mirroring the existing `[tool calls omitted]` rule.
  - New `stripCopiedCurrentMessageScaffolding` removes copied scaffolding blocks. A block is dropped when its first line is a bracketed marker (`[Chat messages …]` / `[Current message - respond to this]`); a plain-text `Current message priority:` line only triggers a drop when the block **also** carries one of those bracketed markers — as real copied scaffolding always does. This keeps an ordinary reply that merely begins with the phrase (e.g. `Current message priority: high means …`) from being erased, consistent with the sanitizer's existing preserve-inline-mentions behavior.
- **`src/auto-reply/reply/normalize-reply.ts`**
  - A bare `(no output)` payload now clears the placeholder text and is skipped with the new `empty-tool-output` reason **only when no other sendable content remains**. When the payload still carries media, presentation, interactive, or channelData, that content is preserved and delivered (gated through the existing `hasContent("")` check), so non-text replies are not dropped.
- Regression tests added for the sanitizer (`embedded-agent-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts`) and the normalizer (`reply-utils.test.ts`): coverage that a bare `(no output)` payload carrying media/channelData still delivers its non-text content, and that an ordinary reply beginning with `Current message priority:` is preserved while a copied scaffold block led by that line is still stripped.

All rules match standalone lines/blocks only, so text that merely mentions or starts with the literal strings inline is preserved — see the `"The literal (no output) text is relevant."` and `"Current message priority: high means …"` cases.

> This re-authors the approach from #78189 (which was closed only for an author PR-queue limit, not on review/design grounds) against current `main`: the helper moved from `pi-embedded-helpers/` to `embedded-agent-helpers/`, and the sanitize pipeline gained `stripAssistantInternalTraceLines` / `stripPlainTextToolCallBlocks` steps since then, so the change is rebased onto the current shape.

## Testing
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts` → **123 + 76 passing** (including the new cases)
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false` → clean
- `oxlint` and `oxfmt --check` on the changed files → clean

## AI-assisted disclosure
This PR was prepared with AI assistance. Per `CONTRIBUTING.md`, the automated tests/typecheck/lint above are supplemental. The real-behavior proof below is a direct run of the production functions at the current PR head; a live-channel recording can be added on request. `CHANGELOG.md` intentionally left untouched per the contributor guidelines.


## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Final user-facing replies could leak internal prompt scaffolding — `[Chat messages since your last reply - for context]`, `[Current message - respond to this]`, `Current message priority: …` — and bare `(no output)` tool placeholders when the model echoed its own input back into the reply text; the reply normalizer must not drop a media/channelData payload whose text is a bare `(no output)`; and the priority-marker rule must not erase an ordinary reply that merely begins with `Current message priority:` (#78177).

**Real environment tested:** Clean checkout at the current PR head `daf946d6` on Windows 11, Node `v24.13.0`. `git rev-parse HEAD` was verified to equal `daf946d6500f…` with a clean working tree immediately before and after the run. The shipped `sanitizeUserFacingText()` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.ts`) and `normalizeReplyPayload()` (`src/auto-reply/reply/normalize-reply.ts`) — the same functions the reply pipeline calls before delivery — were executed directly against the issue inputs and against `(no output)` payloads carrying non-text content. This is a direct run of the production code paths at current head; it is not a full live-channel deployment (happy to add a live channel recording if preferred).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** A harness imports the real exported functions and prints input/output (and skip reasons):

```ts
import { sanitizeUserFacingText } from "./src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.js";
import { normalizeReplyPayload } from "./src/auto-reply/reply/normalize-reply.js";
// sanitizeUserFacingText(...) on scaffolding, priority-prose, and (no output) inputs
// normalizeReplyPayload(payload, { onSkip }) on bare (no output), +mediaUrl, +channelData
```

Command: `node --import tsx _proof_94112.mts` (executed with `HEAD == daf946d6`, tree clean).

**Evidence after fix:** Copied console output from the current-head (`daf946d6`) run:

```text
===== sanitizeUserFacingText (current-message / history scaffolding) =====
OUT: "Pong."
OUT: "Visible reply."

===== sanitizeUserFacingText (priority-marker prose is PRESERVED, not over-stripped) =====
IN : "Current message priority: high means the sender flagged the message as urgent."
OUT: "Current message priority: high means the sender flagged the message as urgent."

===== sanitizeUserFacingText ((no output) placeholder) =====
"(no output)"                                  -> ""
"Before\n(no output)\nAfter"                   -> "Before\nAfter"
"The literal (no output) text is relevant."    -> "The literal (no output) text is relevant."

===== normalizeReplyPayload ((no output) skip vs non-text preservation) =====
--- bare (no output)
  payload: {"text":"(no output)"}
  result : null
  skips  : ["empty-tool-output"]
--- (no output) + mediaUrl
  payload: {"text":"  (no output)\n","mediaUrl":"https://example.com/photo.jpg"}
  result : {"text":"","mediaUrl":"https://example.com/photo.jpg"}
  skips  : []
--- (no output) + channelData
  payload: {"text":"(no output)","channelData":{"line":{"flexMessage":{"type":"bubble"}}}}
  result : {"text":"","channelData":{"line":{"flexMessage":{"type":"bubble"}}}}
  skips  : []
```

**Observed result after fix:** At current head `daf946d6`, `sanitizeUserFacingText` strips the `[Current message - respond to this]` / `[Chat messages since your last reply - for context]` scaffolding (and a `Current message priority:` line when it heads such a block), returning only the genuine reply (`Pong.`, `Visible reply.`). The over-stripping fix is confirmed: an ordinary reply that merely begins with `Current message priority: high means …` is returned unchanged, and the inline mention `The literal (no output) text is relevant.` is also unchanged. Standalone `(no output)` lines are removed (`""`, `Before\nAfter`). `normalizeReplyPayload` skips a bare `(no output)` payload (result `null`, reason `empty-tool-output`); when the same `(no output)` payload also carries `mediaUrl` or `channelData`, the placeholder text is cleared to `""` and the non-text content is preserved and delivered (no skip recorded). Current `main` / `v2026.6.8` lack these sanitizer/normalizer rules, so the identical inputs surface the scaffolding and `(no output)` verbatim.

**What was not tested:** A full end-to-end delivery through a live messaging channel (e.g., a real Telegram bot) was not exercised — verification runs the production functions directly against the issue inputs and payloads.

target.title: fix(config): allow explicit main agent bindings when agents.list is non-empty
target.body:
## Summary

Route bindings to the implicit default agent `"main"` are rejected at config load and silently deleted by `doctor --fix` when `agents.list` is non-empty. This breaks the documented multi-agent config pattern where a few ACP agents are listed while `"main"` handles everything else.

The fix treats `DEFAULT_AGENT_ID` (`"main"`) as a valid explicit binding target in both config validation and doctor pruning, consistent with how routing already uses it as the universal fallback.

Closes #89412

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Config load throws `Unknown agent id "main" (not in agents.list)` and `doctor --fix` silently removes valid `main` bindings from disk when `agents.list` is non-empty.

**Real environment tested**: macOS 26.6 (Darwin arm64), Node v22.22.0, OpenClaw source checkout at `2d61521bd`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# Live verification: schema accepts 'main' as binding target with non-empty agents.list
node --import tsx -e "
import { OpenClawSchema } from './src/config/zod-schema.js';
const config = {
  agents: { list: [{ id: 'codex' }, { id: 'claude' }] },
  bindings: [{ match: { channel: 'webchat' }, agentId: 'main' }]
};
try {
  const parsed = OpenClawSchema.parse(config);
  console.log('PASS: config with main bindings accepted');
} catch (e) {
  console.log('FAIL:', e.issues.slice(0,3).map(i => i.path.join('.') + ': ' + i.message).join('; '));
}
"

# Unit tests
npx tsx node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts
npx tsx node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-legacy-config.migrations.test.ts
npx tsx node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix**:

Live runtime verification (before vs after fix):

```
# BEFORE fix (main branch):
$ node --import tsx -e "..."
BEFORE-FIX: FAIL: bindings.0.agentId: Unknown agent id \"main\" (not in agents.list).

# AFTER fix (this branch):
$ node --import tsx -e "..."
PASS: config with main bindings accepted
```

All existing tests pass — no regressions:

```
 ✓ config.schema-regressions.test.ts — 32 tests passed
 ✓ doctor-legacy-config.migrations.test.ts — 51 tests passed
 ✓ legacy-config-migrate.test.ts — 103 tests passed

 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  186 passed (186)
```

Lint clean: `oxlint` 0 warnings 0 errors across both changed files.

**Observed result after fix**: Configs with `agents.list: [{id: "codex"}, {id: "claude"}]` and `bindings` targeting `agentId: "main"` no longer fail config load and are no longer stripped by `doctor --fix`. Genuinely missing agent ids (not in list and not `"main"`) continue to be rejected/pruned.

**What was not tested**: Full end-to-end gateway startup with the fixed config (local CI only).

@clawsweeper review

target.title: fix(secrets): skip PLAINTEXT_FOUND for apiKey values that look like env var references
target.body:
## Summary

When `apiKey` config fields contain environment variable names (e.g. `OPENCLAW_DEEPSEEK_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`) instead of actual secret values, `openclaw secrets audit --check` still reports PLAINTEXT_FOUND warnings. Users with multiple providers see several false positives on every audit run.

This change adds a heuristic to skip env-var-like values: a string matching `^[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*$` that ends in `_KEY`, `_TOKEN`, `_SECRET`, or `_PASSWORD` is treated as an environment variable reference and skipped.

Closes #87723

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Secrets / audit

**Behavior or issue addressed**: `openclaw secrets audit --check` reports PLAINTEXT_FOUND for apiKey fields whose values are environment variable names (e.g. `OPENCLAW_DEEPSEEK_API_KEY`) instead of actual secret values.

**Real environment tested**: macOS 26.6, Node v22.22.0, OpenClaw 2026.5.27 source checkout

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

1. Configure a model provider with an env-var-like apiKey value in openclaw.json:
```json
{
  "models": {
    "providers": {
      "deepseek": {
        "baseUrl": "https://api.deepseek.com/v1",
        "apiKey": "OPENCLAW_DEEPSEEK_API_KEY"
      }
    }
  }
}
```
2. Run: `openclaw secrets audit --check`

**Evidence after fix**:

Before (on main):
```
[PLAINTEXT_FOUND] warn models.providers.deepseek.apiKey is stored as plaintext.
# exit code: 1
```

After (this PR):
```
// No finding for deepseek.apiKey — recognized as env var reference
# exit code: 0
```

Regression test: `vitest src/secrets/audit.test.ts`
```
 ✓ does not flag bare env-var-style apiKey values as plaintext
 ✓ keeps request headers in openclaw config covered by plaintext audit
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  26 passed (26)
```

**Observed result after fix**: Env-var-style apiKey values (`OPENAI_API_KEY`, `OPENCLAW_DEEPSEEK_API_KEY`) no longer trigger PLAINTEXT_FOUND. Real secret values continue to be flagged as before.

**What was not tested**: Non-ASCII env var names, edge cases with mixed-case suffixes.

@clawsweeper re-review

target.title: fix(ios): reset sidebar navigation stacks
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes the iPad/sidebar navigation regression where a pushed Settings detail could survive after selecting another sidebar root such as Overview, Chat, or Talk.
- Makes embedded sidebar/phone detail views use their owning shell `NavigationStack` instead of creating competing root stacks.
- Keeps the iPhone Control hub safe by clearing its local detail path before handing off to root tabs.
- Keeps the iPad drawer sidebar interactive after a pushed Settings route by moving the dimming tap layer outside the sidebar hit area.
- Out of scope: broader iOS layout redesign, settings content changes, or unrelated stale source guard expectations.

Review focus: `RootTabs` shell ownership, embedded `ownsNavigationStack` usage, drawer hit-testing, and the regression guards in the RootTabs tests.

## Linked context

Closes: none

Related: none

Requested by maintainer during iOS 27 navigation crash/bounce investigation.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: iPad sidebar/drawer navigation could stay stuck on a pushed Settings subpage after selecting Overview; same navigation-shell cleanup was applied to iPhone Control handoff paths.
- Real environment tested: local iOS Simulator via XcodeBuildMCP, iPad profile `OpenClaw iPad Preview` and iPhone 17 Pro profile, screenshot fixture mode.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: launched the app with `--openclaw-screenshot-mode --openclaw-initial-destination settings --openclaw-sidebar-visibility visible`, opened Settings -> Channels / Integrations, opened the sidebar drawer, then tapped Overview.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

After fix, tapping Overview exits the pushed Channels detail and shows the Overview screen:

![After: Settings subpage switches to Overview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/after-ipad-settings-overview.gif)

Raw after recording: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/after-ipad-settings-overview.mp4

After runtime log: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/logs/after-ipad-runtime.log

Physical iOS 27 device install/connect proof captured after the fix:

```text
openclaw nodes status --json
nodeId: cf74bea339636ed9cee784f41e00f650afba207c22ef0efd5045aa2618ed5454
platform: iOS 27.0.0
version: 2026.6.2
clientMode: node
remoteIp: 192.168.1.231
approvalState: approved
paired: true
connected: true
lastSeenReason: connect
caps: 11
commands: 14

openclaw devices list --json
deviceId: cf74bea339636ed9cee784f41e00f650afba207c22ef0efd5045aa2618ed5454
platform: iOS 27.0.0
clientMode: node
role: node
roles: node, operator
tokenRoles: node, operator
lastSeenReason: connect
```

Physical-device runtime note: the gateway log also captured the earlier setup repair failures from the same phone (`token_missing`, `bootstrap_token_invalid`, then `token_mismatch` while stale credentials were being cleared). After reset/re-pair, the current node state above is connected/approved.

- Observed result after fix: semantic UI snapshot after tapping Overview contained `Overview`, `Gateway Healthy`, and no longer showed the Channels detail as the active screen.
- What was not tested: physical iOS 27 install/connect/log proof is now captured; the original Overview bounce/crash tap path is still covered by simulator before/after GIFs rather than a physical-device video.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: local `origin/main` required the repo-standard `pnpm ios:gen` project regeneration before XcodeBuildMCP testing because the generated Xcode project had stale WatchExtension paths after the watch app move.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

Before fix, the same interaction remains on Channels / Integrations after tapping Overview:

![Before: Settings subpage remains stale after Overview tap](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/before-ipad-settings-overview.gif)

Raw before recording: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/before-ipad-settings-overview.mp4

Before runtime log: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/logs/before-ipad-runtime.log

Proof summary: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/proof-summary.md

## Tests and validation

Commands/proof run:

- `pnpm ios:gen`
- XcodeBuildMCP iPad focused tests: `-only-testing:OpenClawTests/RootTabsSidebarRegressionTests -only-testing:OpenClawTests/RootTabsPresentationTests -only-testing:OpenClawTests/SwiftUIRenderSmokeTests` -> 57 passed, 0 failed.
- XcodeBuildMCP iPhone focused tests: same focused suite -> 57 passed, 0 failed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

Regression coverage added/updated:

- Settings embedded under a parent navigation stack.
- Sidebar selection clears the Settings route path before swapping roots.
- iPad drawer dimming layer does not steal sidebar touches.
- iPhone Control hub clears its detail path before root-tab handoff.

Known existing failures/gaps:

- Full `RootTabsSourceGuardTests` had pre-existing stale gateway transport string guard failures during earlier local checks; the touched focused suites above pass.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Sidebar and phone Control navigation now clear stale detail paths instead of preserving pushed subpages across root switches.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

SwiftUI navigation ownership across iPad sidebar, iPhone Control hub, and standalone tab contexts.

How is that risk mitigated?

The default standalone behavior still owns local stacks; only embedded shell contexts opt out. Focused simulator tests and before/after recordings cover both the failing iPad path and the iPhone safety path.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and maintainer review. Physical iOS 27 install/connect proof is captured above; physical-device video of the Overview tap path was not captured.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed the review concern about stale Settings detail screens by binding/resetting the sidebar navigation path, and expanded the fix after live proof showed the drawer dimming layer could still intercept sidebar selection.

AI-assisted: Yes. Local Codex/autoreview was run and returned no accepted/actionable findings.

commands.author: kiranmagic7

target.title: fix(cli): differentiate gateway-restart hint for hot-loadable agent config sets (#80722)
target.body:
## Summary

- `config set`, `config patch`, and `config unset` now derive their post-write hint from the shared gateway reload plan instead of always printing `Restart the gateway to apply.`
- Hot-reloadable config paths, including `agents.list.*`, `agents.defaults.models.*`, and `models.*` runtime-policy edits, now print `Change will apply without restarting the gateway.`
- Restart-required paths still keep `Restart the gateway to apply.`; mixed batches keep the restart hint if any touched path requires restart.
- Legacy whole-agent runtime keys such as `agents.list[].agentRuntime` no longer force a restart hint, matching current runtime-selection docs/source where those keys are ignored by runtime selection.

Closes #80722.

## Why

The old CLI success text was stale after reload metadata landed: `agents.list` and model config are classified as hot-reloadable by the gateway reload plan, but CLI writes still printed the generic restart warning. That made active agent/model changes look restart-required even when they apply without a gateway restart.

The earlier implementation treated legacy per-agent `agentRuntime.id` as load-bearing. Current main has moved runtime selection to provider/model policy, so this refresh aligns the CLI hint with `buildGatewayReloadPlan()` instead of encoding legacy whole-agent runtime semantics in the CLI.

## Implementation

- Added `configApplyHintForPaths()` / `configApplyHintForOperations()` in `src/cli/config-cli.ts`.
- The helper calls `buildGatewayReloadPlan()` with the changed config paths.
- Result mapping:
  - `restartGateway: true` -> `Restart the gateway to apply.`
  - hot reasons present -> `Change will apply without restarting the gateway.`
  - no hot/restart reason -> `No gateway restart needed.`
- `runConfigOperations()` and direct `runConfigUnset()` use the helper for success text.
- Added regression coverage for active agent model writes, legacy whole-agent `agentRuntime`, provider runtime policy, restart-required gateway paths, and mixed hot+restart batches.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** The CLI used to print `Restart the gateway to apply.` after hot-reloadable config writes such as active agent model updates. After this patch, the CLI prints the hot-reload hint for hot paths while keeping the restart hint for restart-required paths.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `0b18f14e4b7fa699f82eae9a98a3a6e81ca828a4`, using the repository's real `node scripts/run-node.mjs config` command against a temporary `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json` fixture.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```console
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config set 'agents.list[0].model.primary' '"openai/gpt-5.4-mini"' --strict-json
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config set 'agents.list[1].model.primary' '"openai/gpt-5.4-mini"' --strict-json
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config set 'models.providers.openai.agentRuntime.id' '"pi"' --strict-json
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config set 'gateway.auth.mode' 'token'
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-80722.fpLUa4/openclaw.json node scripts/run-node.mjs config unset 'agents.list[1].agentRuntime'
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from those live CLI commands:

```console
Updated agents.list.0.model.primary. Change will apply without restarting the gateway.
Updated agents.list.1.model.primary. Change will apply without restarting the gateway.
Updated models.providers.openai.agentRuntime.id. Change will apply without restarting the gateway.
Updated gateway.auth.mode. Restart the gateway to apply.
Removed agents.list[1].agentRuntime. Change will apply without restarting the gateway.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Active-agent model writes, legacy whole-agent runtime removal, and provider runtime-policy edits now avoid the stale restart hint; the restart-required gateway auth path still prints `Restart the gateway to apply.`.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a full long-lived gateway reload loop here; this PR only changes the CLI post-write success hint and reuses the existing gateway reload planner for the classification.

## Verification

```console
$ pnpm exec oxfmt --write src/cli/config-cli.ts src/cli/config-cli.test.ts --threads=1
Finished in 22ms on 2 files using 1 threads.

$ OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/config-cli.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  105 passed (105)

$ git diff --check
# exit 0
```

## Compatibility / risk

Low behavior risk: this only changes CLI success text after config writes. It does not change config parsing, validation, file writes, reload behavior, or gateway runtime selection. The hint now follows the same reload planner used by the gateway, so future reload-rule changes are not duplicated in the CLI.

commands.author: mushuiyu886

target.title: fix #94166: memory-core OpenAI-compatible embeddings: honor provider request.allowPrivateNetwork
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `memory-core` OpenAI-compatible embedding requests did not carry the selected configured provider's `request.allowPrivateNetwork` transport policy into the embedding guarded-fetch policy, so memory embeddings could not honor provider-level private-network trust consistently.
- **Solution:** Reuse the existing provider trust contract at the embedding SSRF policy owner: omitted `request.allowPrivateNetwork` preserves exact-host compatibility, explicit `true` overlays broader private-network trust, and explicit `false` opts out of exact-host private-network trust.
- **What changed:** `ConfiguredEmbeddingProvider` reads `request.allowPrivateNetwork`; OpenAI-compatible memory embeddings return no exact-host SSRF policy for explicit provider denial, keep `allowedHostnames` for omitted config, and add `allowPrivateNetwork: true` only for explicit provider opt-in. Regression coverage now locks all three states.
- **What did NOT change:** Other embedding providers, non-memory model-provider request paths, gateway/channel/session routing, global SSRF helpers, schema/config surface, provider request field names, migrations, and metadata/link-local default protections are unchanged.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Existing configured OpenAI-compatible memory provider aliases keep working by default, operators who intentionally need broader private-network trust can use the existing provider `request.allowPrivateNetwork` knob, and operators who explicitly set `request.allowPrivateNetwork: false` get the same denial semantics as the sibling model-provider transport path.
- Fixes #94166

## Origin / follow-up

N/A — independent root cause.

## Competition / linked PR analysis

- **Linked PR(s):** #94180 (`feat(memory-core): allow private network endpoints for memory embeddings`). The public-contract helper also flagged #92307 through an environment/version term, but #92307 is about exec-approval startup warnings and is not the same embedding/private-network contract.
- **Gap in linked PR(s):** #94180 adds a new memory-specific config surface, `memorySearch.remote.network.dangerouslyAllowPrivateNetwork`, for a trust decision that the selected model provider can already express with `models.providers.<id>.request.allowPrivateNetwork`.
- **Why this patch is better/canonical:** This patch reuses the existing provider transport-trust contract as the canonical source of truth for configured providers instead of introducing a second memory-only knob, while preserving shipped exact-host upgrade behavior for omitted config and honoring explicit provider denial.
- **Local real behavior proof advantage:** The local proof exercises the real `memory status --deep --json` CLI path against a loopback/private OpenAI-compatible endpoint in three states: omitted policy succeeds through exact-host trust with one `/v1/embeddings` request, explicit `request.allowPrivateNetwork: false` is blocked before the endpoint receives any request, and explicit `true` succeeds with one `/v1/embeddings` request.
- **Related open PR scan:** Open PR searches for `request.allowPrivateNetwork`, `memory-core allowPrivateNetwork`, and OpenAI-compatible private embedding endpoints found #94180 as the same-contract alternative; no broader canonical provider-inheritance PR was found besides the current PR.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw memory status --deep` against a configured OpenAI-compatible embedding endpoint on a loopback/private address must preserve exact-host compatibility when provider private-network policy is omitted, must block when the selected provider explicitly sets `request.allowPrivateNetwork: false`, and must honor `request.allowPrivateNetwork: true` for explicit broader private-network trust.
- **Real environment tested:** Live CLI proof was captured in the local PR worktree on Linux, using the real source CLI, the real `memory-core` CLI command, isolated `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`, and a local OpenAI-compatible `/v1/embeddings` HTTP endpoint bound to a loopback/private address with host and port redacted below.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** The same command was run three times with the same endpoint and separate isolated configs: omitted `models.providers.private-embedding-proof.request.allowPrivateNetwork`, explicit `request.allowPrivateNetwork: false`, and explicit `request.allowPrivateNetwork: true`.

```sh
node scripts/run-node.mjs memory status --agent proof --deep --json
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Exact-host compatibility result from the real CLI, with provider `request.allowPrivateNetwork` omitted:

```json
{
  "agentId": "proof",
  "status": {
    "provider": "openai-compatible",
    "requestedProvider": "private-embedding-proof",
    "model": "text-embedding-3-small"
  },
  "embeddingProbe": {
    "ok": true
  }
}
```

Exact-host compatibility summary:

```json
{
  "code": 0,
  "containsEmbeddingsReady": true,
  "serverRequestCount": 1
}
```

Explicit provider denial result from the real CLI:

```json
{
  "agentId": "proof",
  "status": {
    "provider": "openai-compatible",
    "requestedProvider": "private-embedding-proof",
    "model": "text-embedding-3-small"
  },
  "embeddingProbe": {
    "ok": false,
    "error": "Blocked hostname or private/internal/special-use IP address"
  }
}
```

Explicit provider denial summary:

```json
{
  "code": 0,
  "containsGuardBlock": true,
  "containsEmbeddingsReady": false,
  "serverRequestCount": 0
}
```

Provider opt-in overlay result from the real CLI:

```json
{
  "agentId": "proof",
  "status": {
    "provider": "openai-compatible",
    "requestedProvider": "private-embedding-proof",
    "model": "text-embedding-3-small"
  },
  "embeddingProbe": {
    "ok": true
  }
}
```

Provider opt-in overlay summary:

```json
{
  "code": 0,
  "containsEmbeddingsReady": true,
  "serverRequestCount": 1
}
```

Redacted endpoint request observed once in the omitted case and once in the opt-in case; the explicit false case observed zero endpoint requests:

```json
{
  "method": "POST",
  "url": "/v1/embeddings",
  "headers": {
    "host": "<loopback>:<redacted-port>",
    "authorization": "<redacted>",
    "content-type": "application/json",
    "x-proof": "openclaw-pr-94344"
  },
  "body": {
    "model": "text-embedding-3-small",
    "input": ["ping"],
    "dimensions": 3
  }
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The omitted configured provider alias reaches the loopback/private embedding endpoint successfully. The explicit false config is blocked by the guarded-fetch path before the endpoint sees a request. The explicit true config returns `embeddingProbe.ok: true` and reaches the endpoint once.
- **What was not tested:** I did not use a maintainer's real private LAN/VPN embedding service, third-party credentials, Mantis, Telegram, or Desktop proof infrastructure. This PR does not touch Telegram/Desktop/channel delivery; the local proof exercises the relevant memory CLI, configured-provider, and guarded-fetch runtime surface. I did not drive a live metadata/link-local endpoint for safety; metadata/link-local rebinding remains covered by the regression test that resolves an exact-host policy to `169.254.169.254` and expects private/internal blocking when broader opt-in is not set. I did not run `openclaw memory index --force`; `memory status --deep` is the requested memory runtime probe path and reaches the embedding availability check directly.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Review findings addressed

| Finding | Status | Resolution |
| --- | --- | --- |
| RF-001 | Addressed | This update is the author-side response to the current `status: ⏳ waiting on author` label. |
| RF-002 | Fixed/proven | Explicit `models.providers.<id>.request.allowPrivateNetwork: false` now returns no embedding exact-host SSRF policy; the live CLI proof shows `embeddingProbe.ok: false`, the private/internal block message, and zero endpoint requests. |
| RF-003 | Disclosed | Memory embeddings intentionally inherit provider-level private-network trust for explicit `true`; maintainer/security acceptance remains required because this is an SSRF trust-boundary policy. |
| RF-004 | Fixed/disclosed | The focused repair aligns explicit false semantics and tests; maintainer/security review remains the final gate for the provider trust inheritance decision. |
| RF-005 | Fixed/proven | `src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.ts` now honors explicit provider denial before constructing exact-host trust, and the owner-module regression test locks that behavior. |
| RF-006 | Fixed/proven | `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts --reporter=verbose` passed locally with 29/29 tests. |
| RF-007 | Fixed/proven | `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts --reporter=verbose` passed locally with 14/14 tests. |

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test files:** `src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts`; `src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts`
- **Scenario locked in:** Configured provider with `request.allowPrivateNetwork: true` sets `client.ssrfPolicy.allowedHostnames === ["llm.internal"]` and `client.ssrfPolicy.allowPrivateNetwork === true`; omitted config preserves exact-host trust and keeps `allowPrivateNetwork` unset; explicit `request.allowPrivateNetwork: false` returns no embedding SSRF policy and private/internal resolution remains blocked; configured local/private provider aliases work under omitted config; the gateway configured-provider route still reaches the local generic embedding provider.
- **Commands run after this patch:**

```sh
CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts --reporter=verbose
CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

- **Observed local result:** `src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts` passed 29/29 tests; `src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts` passed 14/14 tests in the local PR worktree after the explicit false repair.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The unit tests pin the owner module's SSRF policy construction for false/omitted/true, the gateway test pins the configured provider HTTP embedding route, and the live CLI proof pins the runtime memory-core guarded-fetch path. Together they cover the exact source-of-truth boundary without adding a broader fixture or changing unrelated provider paths.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** security-boundary, auth-provider, compatibility, session-state.
- **Risk explanation:** This touches the security boundary for private-network embedding fetches and the provider/config contract that decides when exact-host or broader private-network trust is allowed. A wrong change could relax SSRF blocking by default, break existing configured local/private aliases, or create a second incompatible memory-only trust surface.
- **Why acceptable:** The diff is limited to the OpenAI-compatible embedding provider and its tests; shipped exact-host trust for omitted configured aliases is preserved; explicit false now denies exact-host private trust; metadata/link-local rebinding remains blocked without broader opt-in; broader private-network access stays behind explicit provider-scoped opt-in; no schema, migration, or new public config knob is introduced.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The provider `request.allowPrivateNetwork` contract is the existing source-of-truth for configured provider private-network transport trust, but the memory OpenAI-compatible embedding adapter previously built its SSRF policy from the embedding base URL without consuming the full configured provider request state. The first PR revision handled explicit `true`, but explicit `false` still fell through to exact-host trust.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The trust decision now lives where the embedding SSRF policy is created: explicit provider denial returns no exact-host policy, omitted config preserves the existing exact-host policy, and explicit provider opt-in overlays broader `allowPrivateNetwork` trust. This fixes source policy construction instead of masking blocked fetches downstream or adding a parallel memory-only override.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High — the patch stays focused on the embedding provider policy and configured-provider embedding test coverage, includes real CLI proof for the runtime path, preserves existing alias compatibility, handles explicit denial, and leaves the final product/security acceptance explicitly called out for maintainer review.
- **Patch quality notes:** Patch-quality warnings are expected and reviewed: the `unknown as EmbeddingProviderCreateOptions["config"]` casts are test fixture casts for partial config objects, not production type suppression; returning `undefined` for explicit false is the intentional denial path and matches the sibling model-provider behavior where no exact-origin trust is attached. No skipped tests, `.only`, lint disables, or runtime broad catch were added.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The canonical contract surface is `models.providers.<id>.request.allowPrivateNetwork` for provider transport trust. This patch makes memory embedding SSRF policy consume that source-of-truth contract before guarded fetch runs; it does not change schema/default behavior, does not add migration or partial override semantics, and intentionally leaves other providers and non-memory request paths out of scope.

commands.author: zats

target.title: ci: gate PR edit automation by changed field
target.body:
## Summary
- gate PR `edited` automation on the fields each workflow actually consumes
- keep ClawSweeper dispatch active for title/body/base edits because it serializes PR title into its activity payload
- skip expensive PR automation for metadata-only edits while preserving title/body/base behavior
- add workflow guard coverage and changed-test routing for the edited-field matrix

## What gets optimized
The optimization is: when a PR is edited, each automation wakes only when that edited field can change the job output.

- `Auto response` (`auto-response`): wakes for title, body, or base edits.
- `ClawSweeper Dispatch` (`dispatch`): wakes for title, body, or base edits.
- `Labeler` (`label`): wakes for title or base edits.
- `Real behavior proof` (`Real behavior proof`): wakes for body or base edits.

Metadata-only edits, such as milestone/assignee/project changes, skip these PR edited-event jobs because none of them read those fields.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts`
- `node scripts/check-workflows.mjs .github/workflows/auto-response.yml .github/workflows/clawsweeper-dispatch.yml .github/workflows/labeler.yml .github/workflows/real-behavior-proof.yml`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts"`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: PR `pull_request_target` `edited` automation now wakes only for fields each workflow reads.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on branch `optimize-pr-edit-automation` at commit `b480e41f2f3`, plus GitHub PR observer/check-rollup inspection for PR #94682.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts
node scripts/check-workflows.mjs .github/workflows/auto-response.yml .github/workflows/clawsweeper-dispatch.yml .github/workflows/labeler.yml .github/workflows/real-behavior-proof.yml
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts"
```

Evidence after fix:
```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  189 passed (189)
zizmor...................................................................Passed
No direct inputs interpolation found in composite run blocks.
autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
```

PR observer outcome matrix from the proposed branch workflow guards:

| PR event | auto-response | dispatch | label | Real behavior proof |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| opened | success | success | success | success |
| title edit | success | success | success | skipped |
| body edit | success | success | skipped | success |
| base edit | success | success | success | success |
| metadata edit | skipped | skipped | skipped | skipped |

Observed result after fix: skipped outcomes are intentional. Title edits do not affect real-behavior proof content, body edits do not affect labels, and metadata edits do not affect any of these automation outputs. Base edits wake all four jobs because base can affect routing, labels, proof context, and dispatch context.

Labeler edited-step outcomes:

| PR edit | `actions/labeler` | `Apply PR size label` | `Apply maintainer or trusted-contributor label` | `Apply beta-blocker title label` | `Apply too-many-prs label` |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| title edit | skipped | skipped | skipped | success | skipped |
| base edit | success | success | skipped | skipped | skipped |
| body edit | skipped | skipped | skipped | skipped | skipped |
| metadata edit | skipped | skipped | skipped | skipped | skipped |
